verbGlossary.ZONED BY: {source} is subdivided into functional zones using {target} element hierarchy (e.g., building zoned into UniFormat Level 2 assemblies such as shell, interiors, services)
verbGlossary.DECOMPOSED BY: {source} is broken into constituent building elements according to {target} assembly structure (e.g., floor level decomposed by UniFormat elements for cost estimation)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} or space type is categorized using {target} taxonomy (e.g., room classified under OmniClass Table 13 space types)
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} or compartment is defined and regulated by {target} or standard (e.g., fire/smoke compartment governed by NFPA 101 life safety requirements or IBC Chapter 7 fire-resistance provisions)
verbGlossary.SEALED PER: Penetration or opening at {target} is firestopped or sealed following the target standard's listed assembly requirements (e.g., pipe penetration sealed per NFPA 251 through-penetration firestop procedures)
verbGlossary.SURVEYED FOR: {source} is inspected or walked for hazards cataloged in {target} classification (e.g., project site surveyed for OSHA Focus Four hazards before work begins)
verbGlossary.POSTED PER: {source} is marked with required warning signage or fall-protection notices as mandated by {target} (e.g., roof level posted per OSHA 1926 Subpart M fall protection requirements)
verbGlossary.GRADED PER: {source} is evaluated for slope, clearance, or surface compliance against {target} (e.g., wet area graded per ADA Standards for Accessible Design Section 403 floor surface requirements)
verbGlossary.DIMENSIONED PER: {source} or space layout is verified against {target} minimum dimensional requirements (e.g., restroom dimensioned per ADA clear floor space and turning radius provisions)
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONED PER: {source} is functionally tested and verified as part of the target certification's commissioning prerequisites (e.g., commissioning zone tested per LEED EAp1 fundamental commissioning requirements)
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: {source}'s installed systems are performance-tested following {target} prescribed protocols (e.g., commissioning zone envelope tested per ASHRAE 90.1 energy code compliance)
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} area is defined using {target} division structure to delineate trade scope (e.g., phase area scoped by MasterFormat divisions to sequence electrical before finishes)
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: Equipment or component at {target} is described by the target specification section's performance and installation requirements (e.g., equipment location specified by Section 23 HVAC installation criteria)
verbGlossary.REFERENCED BY: {source} coordinate or grid intersection is tied to {target} spatial reference schema (e.g., grid line intersection referenced by IFC IfcGridAxis for model coordination)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTED BY: {source}-based metric is broken down by the target classification for comparative analysis (e.g., defect density segmented by MasterFormat division to identify problem trades at each {target})
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source}-based rate metric is compared against {target} published thresholds or industry baselines (e.g., firestopping deficiency rate benchmarked to specification section coverage norms)
